top_requirements.requirement: Production coding fluency, most commonly Python and/or TypeScript/JavaScript/Node — writing real code that ships into customer environments, not prototypes
Multi-year professional engineering experience, most commonly framed as 3-8 years (senior-leaning: 5-6 years is the modal ask; manager/director postings ask 8+)
top_requirements.approx_frequency: 308/424 state an explicit years-of-experience figure; "5" and "6" are the most frequent numbers (85 and 69 occurrences), "3" also common (43)

top_requirements.requirement: Willingness to travel and/or embed on-site with a customer
top_requirements.approx_frequency: 273/424 mention travel explicitly (commonly "up to 25%"); 291/424 use embed/on-site language

top_requirements.requirement: Direct, named customer-facing ownership — described as trusted technical advisor or primary technical point of contact, not a supporting role behind an account owner
Cloud platform fluency (AWS, GCP, and/or Azure), usually named as interchangeable options rather than one required cloud
top_requirements.approx_frequency: AWS 138/424, Azure 118/424, GCP 117/424 — most postings name at least one, many name two or three as acceptable

top_requirements.requirement: LLM/GenAI-specific experience (RAG, agents, fine-tuning, evals) — real and growing but a minority pattern, concentrated in AI-labeled titles
top_requirements.approx_frequency: 178/424 mention LLM/GenAI explicitly; 89/424 have "AI" in the job title

top_requirements.requirement: Data pipeline / distributed-data experience, often Spark-specific at data-platform companies
top_requirements.approx_frequency: 102/424 mention Spark; 112/424 mention data pipelines/ETL generally

top_requirements.requirement: Government/federal/public-sector eligibility, sometimes including active security clearance, concentrated at Palantir, Databricks public-sector tracks, Salesforce Missionforce, and defense-tech companies (Anduril, Vannevar Labs, Applied Intuition, Shield AI, Saronic)
Comfort with undocumented/non-standard customer environments and ambiguous, evolving scope rather than a fixed spec
top_requirements.approx_frequency: qualitative pattern seen across the large majority of postings read in full (Instacart, Cloudflare, Roboflow, Palantir, OpenAI, Twilio all use near-identical phrasing about ambiguity/undocumented systems/evolving objectives)

top_requirements.requirement: Degree requirement is loosely enforced — explicit bachelor's-degree language is rare; most postings substitute 'or equivalent practical experience'
top_requirements.approx_frequency: only 8/424 use explicit bachelor's-degree phrasing without an equivalent-experience escape hatch

top_responsibilities: Embed directly inside one customer's (or a small number of customers') engineering/operations team, working alongside their staff rather than handing off a design to them — the single most consistent framing across companies of every size, from Palantir and Cloudflare to Roboflow and Instacart.

Take a validated proof-of-concept or sales-closed deal from zero to a live, production deployment running on the customer's own infrastructure — explicitly described as the '0-to-1' or 'critical last mile' phase distinct from pre-sales solutioning.

Write and ship production code directly into customer codebases/infrastructure — APIs, integration pipelines, data schemas, error handling, observability, test coverage — under the same engineering rigor (code review, monitoring) as core product engineering, not as a sandboxed demo.
Serve as the primary/named technical point of contact for the account, owning technical success end-to-end rather than being one of several people the customer talks to.

Feed field-derived insight back into the core product/platform roadmap — nearly every posting in the sample includes some version of 'inform the roadmap' or 'shape product direction' as an explicit responsibility, not an incidental side effect.

Run hands-on enablement with the customer's own engineers — workshops, hackathons, pair-programming, or reference implementations — so the customer's team can operate/extend the system after the FDE's involvement narrows or ends.

Navigate the customer's security/compliance review process (InfoSec review, architecture review boards) as part of getting code into their environment, translating compliance requirements into concrete implementation decisions.

At the senior/staff/manager tier: define the technical strategy and playbook for how the FDE function scales across accounts, and manage/coach a team of FDEs, while remaining hands-on for the hardest deployments.

comp_signals.range_text: Base salary $135,000–$200,000/yr (most FDSE reqs); $125,000–$200,000 for one US Government variant; $135,000–$145,000 for New Grad tracks. Explicitly stated as base only — "Total compensation... may also include Restricted Stock units, sign-on bonus and other potential future incentives."
$167,000–$230,000 (Forward Deployed Engineer, entry req); other Cloudflare FDE reqs in the sample range up to $194,000–$266,000 for Senior
$171,120–$213,900 up to $201,280–$251,600 depending on location zone (multiple stated bands in one posting)
$165,000–$247,000 base band (one location); $183,000–$276,000 in others (Senior Customer Forward Deployed Engineer)
Director track: $195,000–$323,000; Manager track: $161,000–$266,000 (Forward-Deployed AI Engineer, AI Mobilization & Transformation)
$160,000–$185,000 (Forward Deployed Engineer, IC); $300,000–$350,000 (Director, Forward Deployed Engineering)
$179,400–$224,250 base salary (San Francisco/New York/Seattle) for Forward Deployed Engineer, GenAI
$148,500–$260,100 (Missionforce Senior/Lead Forward Deployed Engineer, Public Sector)

seniority_distribution_notes: The sample is dominated by mid-to-senior IC roles: 233/424 titles carry no seniority modifier (plain "Forward Deployed Engineer/Software Engineer" — mostly Palantir's core req, which is itself mid-level in practice) and 118/424 are explicitly "Senior." A real but small leadership layer exists above that — Staff (9), Principal (7), Lead (18), Director (8), Manager (24), Head of (2), roughly 16% of postings combined — confirming the brief's staff/principal and pod-lead/practice-lead branch points are real, current hiring patterns, not just historical. A genuine junior/entry pipeline also exists but is small and concentrated almost entirely at Palantir: 16 Internship postings and 5 explicit New Grad postings (21/424, ~5%), consistent with the brief's note that Palantir (and Ramp) will hire with minimal post-college experience while most other companies want 3-8 years.
notable_divergence_from_existing_brief: Two things the fresh sample surfaces that the existing brief understates or frames differently. (1) Government/public-sector/defense FDE work is a large, established sub-track across many companies, not an emerging one-off signaled only by a single OpenAI "FDE, Gov" posting as the brief frames it — 177/424 postings (42%) reference government/federal/public-sector/clearance/defense terms, spanning Palantir's extensive US Government/Intel/Tactical Edge/Warp Speed lines, Databricks' Public Sector and U.S. Federal Sector FDE reqs, Salesforce's Missionforce public-sector track, and a cluster of defense-tech-native companies hiring FDEs as a core title (Anduril, Vannevar Labs, Applied Intuition, Shield AI, Saronic, Hadrian). This looks less like a nascent OpenAI-specific sub-track and more like an already-mature, parallel commercial/defense split within the archetype worth naming explicitly rather than flagging as a "possible v2 refinement." (2) The brief's headline Palantir comp figures ($171K-$295K+ total comp, median $211K, from Levels.fyi) are total-comp figures, while Palantir's own live postings in this sample state base-salary-only ranges of $135K-$200K (new grad $135K-$145K) with equity/bonus called out as additive but unquantified — the two sets of numbers are consistent once reconciled (base + meaningful equity = total comp), but the brief doesn't clearly flag that its cited $171K-$295K+ figure is total comp, not base, which could read as inconsistent with a company's own posted base range if a user cross-checks. Everything else the sample surfaces — no-quota/no-commission comp structure (quota/commission language essentially absent: 1/424 and 1/424 respectively), heavy production-coding emphasis, feedback-to-roadmap responsibility, travel/embed expectations, and the SWE-lateral-hire dominant entry path — confirms the brief closely.

dimension_evidence.note: Postings repeatedly describe undocumented/non-standard customer systems, 'dynamic environment with evolving objectives,' and defining the technical approach as part of the job (Instacart, Cloudflare, Palantir, OpenAI all use near-identical phrasing) — consistent with a high but not maximal target.

Not a strong explicit theme in the corpus either way — postings describe project-paced work (workshops, deployment phases) rather than an inbound-queue/paging cadence, consistent with a moderate, low-weighted target.

No postings in the sample mention formal on-call/paging rotations; 'production monitoring' is named as a responsibility but framed as engineering rigor, not incident response — consistent with a low target.

Postings mention troubleshooting/observability/test coverage as real but routine engineering practice, not urgent multi-layer diagnosis under an SLA clock the way an SRE/infra brief would describe — moderate target looks right.

Postings describe architecting solutions and integration pipelines scoped to one customer's deployment, not platforms meant to serve many consuming teams or scale multiple orders of magnitude — moderate, not high, target is well supported.

Strong support: AWS/GCP/Azure are each named in 27-33% of postings usually as interchangeable options, plus wide language breadth (Python, TypeScript, Java, C++ all appear as acceptable), plus postings spanning verticals (finance, healthcare, manufacturing, defense, retail) for the same company — consistent with high breadth-over-depth target.

Very strong, direct support: repeated 'own production rollouts,' 'own the account's technical success,' 'own end-to-end delivery outcomes' language across companies (Databricks, Cloudflare, OpenAI) — the max-target, max-weight framing holds up well.

Strongly supported: 241/424 postings use explicit customer/client-facing phrasing, plus workshops, hackathons, live demos, and being the 'primary technical point of contact' — counterparts described are engineering/operations teams, not purely executive, consistent with a high-but-not-maximal target.

Mostly supported (pair-programming/enablement so a customer team can self-maintain is private/1:1, matching a low target), but one recurring counter-example is worth flagging: several Databricks AI-FDE postings explicitly list 'present at conferences... recognized as a thought leader internally and externally' as a responsibility, which leans more public/one-to-many than the target-2 framing assumes. Minority pattern, not majority.

Directly confirms the brief's already-documented v1.1 finding: the corpus shows genuine bimodality (Palantir/Anduril/Vannevar single strategic-client embeds vs. Databricks/Cresta/Twilio/Instacart naming multiple accounts/portfolios), so the lowered 0.5 weight to reflect real uncertainty looks appropriate rather than over- or under-confident.

Supported across both breadth modes: 'long-term technical partner,' 'trusted technical advisor,' sustained embeds are used even by the portfolio-style companies, matching the brief's finding that continuity stays high regardless of breadth.

Very strong support: quota/commission language is essentially absent from the corpus (1/424 for each term, both false leads on manual check), while equity/RSU language appears in 170/424 — comp structure reads as core-SWE (base + equity + bonus), not sales-attainment-linked, across companies of every size sampled.

Very strong support: 'write and ship production code,' 'hands-on contributor... not just an advisor,' and explicit language/tooling requirements (Python 306/424, TypeScript/JS 230/424) recur across nearly every company read in full.

Well supported: 273/424 postings mention travel (commonly 'up to 25%'), 291/424 use embed/on-site language; several postings require 100% on-site presence (e.g., Handshake Staff FDE: '5 days/week in-office'), consistent with a high target.

Mostly not discussed in the sample; a small defense-tech-adjacent minority (Anduril Air Defense/Mission Autonomy, Vannevar Labs, Shield AI, Saronic — roughly 15/424 postings) works in domains where adversarial framing is plausible but the posting text itself doesn't foreground threat-modeling as a job skill, so the low target/low weight for the archetype overall is still reasonable.

Mostly absent from the sample, but a small edge/hardware-adjacent pattern exists worth noting: Roboflow FDE explicitly mentions configuring edge devices, and Palantir has dedicated Edge Autonomous Systems / Mixed Reality / Tactical Edge FDSE lines. Still a minority of the corpus, so the low overall target holds, but this is the dimension with the thinnest margin of support.

Supported: the large majority of titles are IC-focused (233 unlabeled + 118 Senior), while a real but minority management/director/head-of layer exists (34/424, ~8%) reflecting the pod-lead branch point the brief describes — consistent with a low-but-nonzero target.

Mostly supported (customer-embedded work reads as private to the account), but the same Databricks 'present at conferences/thought leader' language noted under teaching_enjoyment is a direct counter-example worth the cross-review team's attention, even though it's a minority pattern and the dimension carries a low weight already.

The 'real adjacency, not the modal case' framing is empirically about right: 178/424 (42%) mention LLM/GenAI and 89/424 have 'AI' in the title — substantial and clearly growing, but still a minority of the full posting set, which still includes plenty of non-AI-flavored FDSE reqs at Palantir and others.
No evidence of mobile-specific requirements anywhere in the sample; low target is well supported.

Supported: Spark appears in 102/424 (24%, concentrated at Databricks) and data pipeline/ETL language in 112/424 (26%) — a real, secondary skill pattern rather than the archetype's defining trait, consistent with the low-secondary weighting.

Supported: AWS/GCP/Azure are each named in roughly a quarter to a third of postings and Kubernetes/Docker appear in a smaller minority (36/424 and 19/424) — cloud fluency is commonly required but framed as in-service of the customer deployment, not a deep infra specialization, matching the secondary target/weight.
